findings.problem: 'Calibrated' or 'calibration' appears 4 times in this chapter (full-novel count: 30). The word is thematically motivated — Simone is operating a system that literally calibrates — but at this frequency in a single chapter it has become the dominant note rather than one element of the diction. A shortlist reader would hear the narrator announcing the theme rather than embodying it.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep 1 instance — the most directly technical one, where Simone is literally adjusting the AI's parameters. Replace the other 3 at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:1592, :1606, :1620, or :1638. Strategy options: (a) use Simone's established UX vocabulary ('tuned,' 'adjusted,' 'configured,' 'weighted') to vary the tech-metaphor diction, (b) in emotional-register contexts, replace with psychological verbs ('measured,' 'gauged,' 'performed').
findings.acceptance_test: Revised chapter contains no more than 1 instance of 'calibrated'/'calibration.' Replacement words are drawn from Simone's established UX/design vocabulary. No replacement introduces a new single-word tic by repeating the same substitute more than once.

non_negotiables: No more than 1 instance of 'calibrated'/'calibration' in the revised chapter
The retained instance must be the most directly technical use where Simone is literally adjusting AI parameters
No replacement word is repeated more than once (avoid creating a new single-word tic)
acceptance_targets: Three of the four instances at lines 1592, 1606, 1620, 1638 are replaced
Replacement words are drawn from Simone's UX/design vocabulary ('tuned,' 'adjusted,' 'configured,' 'weighted') or psychological verbs ('measured,' 'gauged,' 'performed')
Each replacement uses a different substitute word
